correctness.justification: All 17/17 acceptance tests pass. The implementation correctly handles all specified requirements: atomic writes via temp file + rename, text/bytes content, parent directory creation, permission preservation, and proper cleanup.

edge_cases.justification: All edge cases handled: empty string/bytes content, TypeError for non-str/bytes input, no stray temp files after success, permission preservation on overwrite, nested directory creation, encoding ignored for bytes. All 17 acceptance tests pass including edge cases.

code_quality.justification: Excellent use of modern Python type hints (str | Path, int | None), clean structure, good naming. The unused `mode` parameter is slightly awkward but documented. Proper use of Path, os.replace(), and context managers. Minor: could use a single NamedTemporaryFile call with conditional mode.

documentation.justification: Comprehensive module and function docstrings with clear Args and Raises sections. Explains the atomic write strategy, notes the `mode` parameter's API compatibility purpose, and documents encoding behavior for bytes. Minor gap: no usage example in docstring.

test_coverage.justification: 13 tests covering happy path (text, bytes, str path), edge cases (empty content, nested dirs, overwrite), error paths (TypeError for int and list), and specific behaviors (permission preservation, no temp file leakage, encoding with bytes). Good variety. Minor gap: no test for OSError scenarios or very large content.

robustness.justification: Validates content type with descriptive error message, creates parent directories, preserves existing permissions, cleans up temp files in finally block even on failure, uses os.replace() for atomic rename. Minor: doesn't validate path type explicitly (though Path() constructor handles it), and OSError in chmod after rename isn't caught.
